reviews.review_description: TELL ME WHY I BARLEY TAP THE BALL AND IT GOES FLYING FOR NO REASON MOST Unrealistic SOCCER GAME I HAVE EVER SEEN. PLAYERS GOT THE STAMINA OF A 85 YEAR OLD

developer_response.body: To be honest, we don’t fully understand exactly what happened with the ball or which control mode you were using, so more details would be really helpful for us to review it properly. Motion Soccer includes several control styles and gameplay options, and we keep improving the experience based on player feedback. If you can explain a bit more about what you were doing when this happened, we’ll be happy to take a closer look.

reviews.review_description: literally everything about it is terrible . unrealistic controls , players run into you and then it’s a pen for them???? i can’t even dribble for 5 seconds without the game randomly smashing the ball up the pitch . game is so bad .

developer_response.body:
Motion Soccer is a very physical VR football game, so controls can feel unusual at first, but what you describe about penalties and the ball suddenly being cleared up the pitch definitely sounds like something we should review.

We are constantly improving the game with player feedback, so if you can send us a short video or explain exactly which control mode you were using, it would really help us understand what happened and fix it if something is wrong.

You can contact us at support@ivanovichgames.com or through our Discord. Thanks for giving it a try.

reviews.review_description: w

This game is so butt do not download.￼ The automatically switches you to goalkeeper and you can’t even move nor can you save anything. Everything cost money and you can’t even play the real game correctly. This game is absolutely horrible and I hope the developers have a downfall cause this game is so trash 🗑️

developer_response.body:
We’re sorry your experience was this frustrating. That’s clearly not the feeling we want anyone to have.

Some of the issues you mention were addressed in later updates, especially around goalkeeper control and movement, and there are options to prevent automatic goalkeeper switching.

That said, we understand first impressions matter a lot, and if the game didn’t click for you at that moment, that’s on us.

We keep improving the game based on feedback, and comments like this help us identify where frustration is coming from.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, joyce.11! We’re really happy you love the refereeing mode. If you’re enjoying the game, a 5-star rating would really help us a lot, especially as we keep improving Motion Soccer with new updates. Thanks for playing and supporting us!

reviews.review_description: Its fun but wish u could refere everything and the pro shouldt be money but ovr 6/10
REALLY? I'M FIVE MINUTES IN THE GAME AND I NEED TO DROP 20 BUCKS TO PLAY LIKE VRFS??? THIS GAME SUCKS

developer_response.body:
It looks like there may be a misunderstanding. The free version already lets you play a lot of the game, including unlimited friendly matches and the Ref Mode with no restrictions.

The PRO version unlocks additional content and features such as Chaos Lab and the Expert control mode, which has a steeper learning curve and is designed for players who want a deeper experience while also supporting the indie project behind the game.

If you give the free version a bit more time, you’ll see there’s already quite a lot to play without paying anything.

reviews.review_description: I found the game very interesting and it will be a great game to play with friends

The game is fun but the AI in the game sucks and can't score a goal themselves and when I try to pass to a another player it sends them somewhere else and the ball remains with me and there is a lot of stick drift and in referee mode when I try to give someone a card the game continues without me giving a card

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for playing and for the feedback!

About passing: in the options you can enable laser passing instead of automatic passing. With laser passing, you choose the direction yourself, so the ball should go exactly where you aim instead of the game deciding the target automatically.

About the stick drift, I’m not completely sure what you mean. If the player moves without touching the stick, it could be related to the controller calibration or hardware drift, but we’d need more details to understand it properly.

About the cards in referee mode: you need to grab the card with the trigger and then raise it toward the player you want to show it to. The game should only continue once the action is completed.

We are still working hard on the game and improving it with player feedback. If you want to help us keep improving Motion Soccer, 5-star reviews really help a lot. And if you can join our Discord and explain the issues in more detail, that would be extremely useful for us.

reviews.review_description: ITs good that u can be a ref
Its good but you shouldn't have to buy stuff like pro for 20 dollars over all 6.5/10

developer_response.body: Thanks for trying the free version and for the honest feedback. We know PRO is not for everyone, but the free version exists so anyone can jump in and enjoy the game first. We’re a small team and we’ve been putting a lot of work into constant updates and improvements. If you’ve had fun with the game already, we hope you might consider giving it 4 stars — or even 5 if you’d like to support us and help more players discover Motion Soccer

reviews.review_description: Its a great game but most of the stuff costs money

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your review and for trying the free version. We’re really glad you like the game. The free version is meant to let players try Motion Soccer and enjoy a part of the experience before deciding whether they want the full version.

If you feel the game deserves it after playing more, updating your review to 5 stars would really help us a lot. It makes a big difference for a small team like ours and helps us keep improving the game with more updates and content. Thanks for your support!

reviews.review_description:
It costs 20$ just to play like vrfs? Back then it used to be free.
.

developer_response.body: The control mode you’re referring to is Expert Control, and yes, it is exclusive to the PRO version. We consider it a more advanced control style, while the free version is designed as an easier way to get started with the game. The free version is still free, so a 1-star review based only on PRO having extra features feels unfair. If this was a misunderstanding, we’d really appreciate it if you could update the review.

reviews.review_title: I LOVE LOVE LOVE this game
reviews.review_description: So much fun you can be ref and players
I accidently bump into another player a little bit and they go flying and ref calls a foul and i can barely make saves don't waste any money on this game

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback — you’re right about the fouls. In version 1.9.1 there was a bug that made collisions trigger exaggerated reactions and overly sensitive referee decisions.

We’ve released update 1.9.2 today, which fixes this issue and improves both collision behavior and referee balance.

Also, just to clarify, this review refers to the free version — there’s no need to spend anything to try it. If you’re willing to give it another shot with the latest update, we’d really appreciate it.

We’re actively improving the game based on feedback like yours. ⚽

This game is  good but the only bad thing is paying for pro

developer_response.body:
Thanks for playing Motion Soccer and for the honest review! ⚽

I completely understand your point. The reason we made a PRO version was not to lock the game behind a paywall, but to be able to keep the free version unusually complete.

In fact, the free version already includes a lot of content, while PRO mainly adds extra content like Season mode and helps support continued development.

If you feel the free version already offers good value, I’d really appreciate it if you considered changing your review to 5 stars. Reviews like that make a big difference and help us continue offering a strong free version.

Thanks again!

reviews.review_description: Firstly everything costs money so you can’t experience it all without paying a ridiculous price secondly referee is the only good mode the rest either doesn’t work cause when the ball gets shot into your net it automatically switches you to goal keeper but you have no time to process that and it goes in and with the pass and shoot mechanic that is trash it’s impossible to win the match and Thirdly every time i tried to move forward my character would do a random 360 before moving forward or any direction and when I have the ball the AI somehow get it by touching my a33 from behind. please do not waste your time on this game unless you want to play referee mode the rest is garbage!

developer_response.body:
A couple of the things you mention are actually optional settings. For example, the automatic goalkeeper possession can be disabled in the options if you do not like that behaviour.

Also, if the default passing with A does not feel good to you, you can try the laser-guided passing control mode instead, where you choose much more directly where you want to pass the ball.

In any case, it is also normal not to start winning matches on the first try while you are still getting used to the controls. Motion Soccer does have a learning curve, even though we have tried to simplify the controls as much as possible.

Thanks again for the feedback. We are always listening and trying to improve the experience.

reviews.review_title: DEV PLS READ!EDITED TO GIVE DEV SPECIFIC
reviews.review_description:
Amazing game. I was confused at first abt how to pass it but if u turn on hand laser in settings it’s much easier. One thing I recommend is make the goalie experience be the same as final soccer. Because the only thing I don’t like about final soccer is u can’t play a whole game so I got motion soccer and then found out the goalie experience is horrible! PLS FIX  

EDIT
The things that are different are the punches
Don’t work nearly as well and the hands r jst a lot clunkier. Also the diving is rlly weird in first person. If that last one can’t be fixed that’s fine I would jst love the punches and hands to work like the did in final soccer. I don’t rlly want to join a discord 

IF YOU FIX ALL THESE THINGS THIS GAME WILL BE AMAZING!!!!! IT WOULD BE SO MUCH BETTER THAT VRFS!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the extra details! What you mention is interesting, and it might help us understand what you’re feeling. In Final Soccer many of the shots were pre-recorded (especially the early ones), so the difficulty was naturally more controlled. In Motion Soccer everything is fully real-time and the goal is full-size, so the saves can end up being much harder.

Because of that, it’s possible that this difference in difficulty creates the impression that the punches or hands behave differently — even though the underlying system is the same. But we’ll definitely take a closer look based on your feedback.

It’s a pity you don’t want to join Discord — we’re currently discussing goalkeeper improvements there and your input would be very valuable — but thank you for taking the time to explain what you felt. We really appreciate it, and we’ll keep working on making the goalkeeper experience feel better. 🙌

developer_response.body: Thanks for the comment, and we’re glad you like the game! In the next version, coming in a few days, we’ve completely redesigned how VAR appears, so it should feel much smoother. We’re working hard to improve Motion Soccer, and getting that missing star back would really help us keep going. Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_title: W soccer game...if you have money
reviews.review_description: You have to pay for everything game play is alright

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and for trying the free version 🙌
Just to clarify how the game is structured: the FREE version already includes a lot of content. You can play unlimited matches as a player, coach, or even referee, with no time limits, and you can also join the online events completely for free, with no restrictions.

If you enjoy the game and want more, the PRO version is a one-time purchase (no subscriptions, no recurring payments) and unlocks additional content like Career Mode, Challenges, more training modes, and Expert Control. Those are kept exclusive simply to give players a clear reason to support the game.

We’re always open to feedback though — if there’s something specific you feel should be included in the free version, feel free to let us know. We really appreciate you giving the game a chance ❤️

reviews.review_description: Kinda bad

developer_response.body:
Thanks for trying Motion Soccer! “Kinda bad” hurts our little developer hearts a bit 😅 but it’s also hard for us to know what to improve from that alone.

Motion Soccer is a very unusual VR football game, with different control styles, player mode, referee mode, tournaments, chaos options, and a lot of settings that can completely change the experience. If something felt bad to you, we’d genuinely love to know what it was.

We’re a small team, but we update the game constantly based on player feedback. If you give it another try and tell us what didn’t work for you, we’ll do our best to improve it.

reviews.review_description: Good game
Its a good game but its kind of hard calling out injured players
they should have a tutorial on how to ref because i don't know how to ref

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback — that’s a totally fair point 👍

Referee mode is intentionally designed to be fast and playable without a long tutorial, but we agree that the first contact can be confusing if you jump in without context.

We’re currently improving in-game hints and onboarding to make referee actions clearer from the very first match. Your comment helps confirm that this is something worth improving.

If you decide to try it again after these changes, we’d love to know if it feels better.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback. Referee Mode is definitely in the game: there is a big button in the main menu called “Referee Mode” that lets you referee a match.

So it’s not edited footage and you don’t need to be lied to about the game name 🙂 You may have simply missed that option in the menu.

If you give it a try and enjoy it, we’d really appreciate it if you updated your review.

reviews.review_description: Like i be goalie and i see my house when i am close to the goal

developer_response.body:
Thanks for pointing that out — that was related to Mixed Reality goalkeeper mode, which could show your real environment near the goal.

This has since been changed and Mixed Reality is now off by default, so you’ll always see the standard VR stadium unless you manually enable it.

We agree it could feel strange, and that’s why we adjusted it.
